params.name: Jadeite Cabbage
params.description:
On Jan 17, 1889 AD, the Jadeite Cabbage was presented to the Qing court.
Legend has it that this jade carving was part of the dowry of Imperial Noble Consort Wenjing. Its lifelike shape resembles a real Chinese cabbage, and the two insects resting on the cabbage leaves are also intricately carved in a realistic way, and the details are very exquisite. As the subject is quite down-to-earth, it is widely loved by the public and became the number one star of the Taipei Palace Museum collection.
